mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate labor income and consumption demand, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window when PAYEMS grows month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 5.0 percent indicates excess labor market slack that reduces household income growth and corporate revenue expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 5.0% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) and restrictive federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) raise real discount rates and input costs, jointly eroding profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when both CPIAUCSL is rising and FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) above trend during early-cycle recovery phases signals accelerating demand with spare capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly higher during early-cycle quarters with GDPC1 growth than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects rising manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which supports higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window when INDPRO expands month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1.8 million units signals contraction in residential investment and construction employment, which drags down aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1800 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint level of unemployment (UNRATE) and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) interacts to compress equity risk premiums: higher unemployment alongside higher rates simultaneously depresses consumption and raises discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of weeks when UNRATE exceeds 5.0% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels (CPIAUCSL) above 195 indicate elevated inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and triggers valuation multiple compression, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 195 over any 3-year rolling window.
